title: Top 5 Geopolitics Headlines

slides.headline: Cairo Takeaway Legal Ruling
slides.explanation: A federal judge ruled that activist Ofir Birenbaum broke terms of a settlement with the Sydney restaurant. This follows a dispute that started when he worked with the Daily Telegraph.

slides.headline: Two Dan Sullivans on Ballot
slides.explanation: Alaska’s Supreme Court decided a man can run against Senator Dan Sullivan even though they share the same name and party. Election officials must now put the challenger on the primary ballot.

slides.headline: Director Sentenced for Fraud
slides.explanation: Hollywood director Carl Rinsch got two and a half years in prison for taking $11 million from Netflix. He claimed the money was for a sci-fi series but used it for himself instead.

slides.headline: Mixed Supreme Court Results
slides.explanation: Donald Trump saw both wins and losses in court today. While one ruling gave him more power to change regulators, he faced significant setbacks on other legal matters.

slides.headline: Trump Demands Lower Gas Prices
slides.explanation: The US president has warned petrol retailers to cut their prices right away. He claims that illegal price gouging is happening and warned of consequences if costs stay high.

caption: From courtrooms to the gas pump, here are five big stories making headlines today. We break down the latest updates so you can stay informed without the noise. Tap through to catch up on what you need to know. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

title: Top 5 Things In Tech Today

slides.headline: California teams up with Anthropic
slides.explanation: California is getting a discount on Anthropic's AI tools. Meanwhile, the federal government is taking a tougher stance against the company.

slides.headline: Is a touchscreen MacBook coming?
slides.explanation: Rumors are swirling about a new Apple laptop with a touchscreen. People are waiting to see if these reports about new chips and features pan out.

slides.headline: Google warns about EU rules
slides.explanation: Google security experts say proposed EU competition rules could put user data at risk. They worry opening up Search and Android might create privacy gaps.

slides.headline: Google limits Meta's AI access
slides.explanation: Google reportedly put a cap on how much Meta can use its Gemini AI for coding projects. It seems like a simple case of not having enough capacity to go around.

slides.headline: Base44 builds its own AI
slides.explanation: The coding platform Base44 just released its own AI model. They hope it will eventually be better than the big models currently on the market.

caption: From government AI deals to rumors about new MacBooks, there is a lot happening in the tech world today. Get the full scoop on these stories and more by checking out the Headlinne app. Stay informed without the noise. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
